title: Scope
text:
Scope. The provisions of this chapter and NFPA 70 shall govern the design, construction, erection and installation of the electrical components, appliances, equipment and systems used in buildings and structures covered by this code. The International Fire Code, the International Property Maintenance Code and NFPA 70 shall govern the use and maintenance of electrical components, appliances, equipment and systems. The International Existing Building Code and NFPA 70 shall govern the alteration, repair, relocation, replacement and addition of electrical components, appliances, or equipment and systems.

Cross-references: NFPA 70; International Fire Code; International Property Maintenance Code; International Existing Building Code.

title: General — Emergency and Standby Power Systems
text:
General. Emergency power systems and standby power systems shall comply with Sections 2702.1.1 through 2702.1.8.

Cross-references: Sections 2702.1.1 through 2702.1.8.

title: Stationary generators
text:
Stationary generators. Stationary emergency and standby power generators required by this code shall be listed in accordance with UL 2200.

Cross-references: UL 2200.

title: Fuel-line piping protection
text:
Fuel-line piping protection. Fuel lines supplying a generator set inside a high-rise building shall be separated from areas of the building other than the room the generator is located in by one of the following methods:

1. A fire-resistant pipe-protection system that has been tested in accordance with UL 1489. The system shall be installed as tested and in accordance with the manufacturer's installation instructions, and shall have a rating of not less than 2 hours.
2. An assembly that has a fire-resistance rating of not less than 2 hours.
3. Other approved methods.

Exception to Condition 1: Where the building is protected throughout with an automatic sprinkler system installed in accordance with Section 903.3.1.1, the required rating shall be reduced to 1 hour.
Exception to Condition 2: Where the building is protected throughout with an automatic sprinkler system installed in accordance with Section 903.3.1.1, the required fire-resistance rating shall be reduced to 1 hour.

Cross-references: UL 1489; Section 903.3.1.1.

title: Installation
text:
Installation. Emergency power systems and standby power systems required by this code or the International Fire Code shall be installed in accordance with the International Fire Code, NFPA 70, NFPA 110 and NFPA 111.

Cross-references: International Fire Code; NFPA 70; NFPA 110; NFPA 111.

title: Load transfer
text:
Load transfer. Emergency power systems shall automatically provide secondary power within 10 seconds after primary power is lost. Standby power systems shall automatically provide secondary power within 60 seconds after primary power is lost.

Exception: Unless specified otherwise in this code.

title: Load duration
text:
Load duration. Emergency power systems and standby power systems shall be designed to provide the required power for a minimum duration of 2 hours without being refueled or recharged.

Exception: Unless specified otherwise in this code.

title: Uninterruptable power source
text: Uninterruptable power source. An uninterrupted source of power shall be provided for equipment where required by the manufacturer's instructions, the listing, this code or applicable referenced standards.

title: Interchangeability
text: Interchangeability. Emergency power systems shall be an acceptable alternative for installations that require standby power systems.

title: Group I-2 occupancies — essential electrical systems in flood hazard areas
text:
Group I-2 occupancies. In Group I-2 occupancies located in flood hazard areas established in Section 1612.3, where new essential electrical systems are installed, and where new essential electrical system generators are installed, the systems and generators shall be located and installed in accordance with ASCE 24. Where connections for hookup of temporary generators are provided, the connections shall be located at or above the elevation required in ASCE 24.

Cross-references: Section 1612.3; ASCE 24.

title: Where required
text:
Where required. Emergency and standby power systems shall be provided where required by Sections 2702.2.1 through 2702.2.19.

Cross-references: Sections 2702.2.1 through 2702.2.19.

title: Ambulatory care facilities
text:
Ambulatory care facilities. Essential electrical systems for ambulatory care facilities shall comply with Section 422.6.

Cross-references: Section 422.6.

title: Elevators and platform lifts
text:
Elevators and platform lifts. Standby power shall be provided for elevators and platform lifts as required in Sections 1009.4.1, 1009.5, 3003.1, 3007.8 and 3008.8.

Cross-references: Section 1009.4.1; Section 1009.5; Section 3003.1; Section 3007.8; Section 3008.8.

title: Emergency responder communication coverage systems
text:
Emergency responder communication coverage systems. Standby power shall be provided for in-building 2-way emergency responder communication coverage systems required in Section 918 and the International Fire Code. The standby power supply shall be capable of operating the in-building 2-way emergency responder communication coverage system at 100-percent system operation capacity for a duration of not less than 12 hours.

Cross-references: Section 918; International Fire Code.

title: Emergency voice/alarm communication systems
text:
Emergency voice/alarm communication systems. Standby power shall be provided for emergency voice/alarm communication systems in accordance with NFPA 72.

Cross-references: NFPA 72.

title: Exhaust systems
text:
Exhaust systems. Standby power shall be provided for common exhaust systems for domestic kitchens located in multistory structures as required in Section 505.5 of the International Mechanical Code. Standby power shall be provided for common exhaust systems for clothes dryers located in multistory structures as required in Section 504.11 of the International Mechanical Code and Section 614.11 of the International Fuel Gas Code.

Cross-references: Section 505.5 of the International Mechanical Code; Section 504.11 of the International Mechanical Code; Section 614.11 of the International Fuel Gas Code.

title: Exit signs
text:
Exit signs. Emergency power shall be provided for exit signs as required in Section 1013.6.3. The system shall be capable of powering the required load for a duration of not less than 90 minutes.

Cross-references: Section 1013.6.3.

title: Gas detection system
text:
Gas detection system. Emergency or standby power shall be provided for gas detection systems in accordance with the International Fire Code.

Cross-references: International Fire Code.

title: Group I-2 occupancies — essential electrical systems
text:
Group I-2 occupancies. Essential electrical systems for Group I-2 occupancies shall be in accordance with Section 407.11.

Cross-references: Section 407.11.

title: Group I-3 occupancies — power-operated doors and locks
text:
Group I-3 occupancies. Emergency power shall be provided for power-operated doors and locks in Group I-3 occupancies as required in Section 408.4.2.

Cross-references: Section 408.4.2.

title: Hazardous materials
text:
Hazardous materials. Emergency or standby power shall be provided in occupancies with hazardous materials where required by the International Fire Code.

Cross-references: International Fire Code.

title: High-rise buildings
text:
High-rise buildings. Emergency and standby power shall be provided in high-rise buildings as required in Section 403.4.8.

Cross-references: Section 403.4.8.

title: Hydrogen fuel gas rooms
text:
Hydrogen fuel gas rooms. Standby power shall be provided for hydrogen fuel gas rooms as required by the International Fire Code.

Cross-references: International Fire Code.

title: Laboratory suites
text:
Laboratory suites. Standby or emergency power shall be provided in accordance with Section 5004.7 of the International Fire Code where laboratory suites are located above the sixth story above grade plane or located in a story below grade plane.

Cross-references: Section 5004.7 of the International Fire Code.

title: Means of egress illumination
text:
Means of egress illumination. Emergency power shall be provided for means of egress illumination as required in Section 1008.3. The system shall be capable of powering the required load for a duration of not less than 90 minutes.

Cross-references: Section 1008.3.

title: Membrane structures
text:
Membrane structures. Standby power shall be provided for auxiliary inflation systems in permanent membrane structures as required in Section 3102.8.2. Standby power shall be provided for a duration of not less than 4 hours. Auxiliary inflation systems in temporary air-supported and air-inflated membrane structures shall be provided in accordance with Section 3103.9.4 of the International Fire Code.

Cross-references: Section 3102.8.2; Section 3103.9.4 of the International Fire Code.

title: Semiconductor fabrication facilities
text:
Semiconductor fabrication facilities. Emergency power shall be provided for semiconductor fabrication facilities as required in Section 415.11.11.

Cross-references: Section 415.11.11.

title: Smoke control systems
text:
Smoke control systems. Standby power shall be provided for smoke control systems as required in Sections 404.7, 909.11, 909.20.6.2 and 909.21.5.

Cross-references: Section 404.7; Section 909.11; Section 909.20.6.2; Section 909.21.5.

title: Special purpose horizontal sliding, accordion or folding doors
text:
Special purpose horizontal sliding, accordion or folding doors. Standby power shall be provided for special purpose horizontal sliding, accordion or folding doors as required in Section 1010.3.3. The standby power supply shall have a capacity to operate not fewer than 50 closing cycles of the door.

Cross-references: Section 1010.3.3.

title: Underground buildings
text:
Underground buildings. Emergency and standby power shall be provided in underground buildings as required in Section 405.

Cross-references: Section 405.

title: Critical circuits
text:
Critical circuits. Required critical circuits shall be protected using one of the following methods:

1. Cables, used for survivability of required critical circuits, that are listed in accordance with UL 2196 and have a fire-resistance rating of not less than 1 hour.
2. Electrical circuit protective systems having a fire-resistance rating of not less than 1 hour. Electrical circuit protective systems are installed in accordance with their listing requirements.
3. Construction having a fire-resistance rating of not less than 1 hour.

Cross-references: UL 2196.

title: Maintenance
text:
Maintenance. Emergency and standby power systems shall be maintained and tested in accordance with the International Fire Code.

Cross-references: International Fire Code.

title: Lightning Protection Systems — General
text:
Lightning Protection Systems — General. Where provided, lightning protection systems shall comply with Sections 2703.2 through 2703.3.

Cross-references: Sections 2703.2 through 2703.3.

title: Lightning Protection — Installation
text:
Installation. Lightning protection systems shall be installed in accordance with NFPA 780 or UL 96A.

Restriction: UL 96A shall not be utilized for buildings used for the production, handling or storage of ammunition, explosives, flammable liquids, flammable gases or other explosive ingredients including dust.

Cross-references: NFPA 780; UL 96A.

title: Surge protection
text:
Surge protection. Where lightning protection systems are installed, surge protective devices shall also be installed in accordance with NFPA 70 and either NFPA 780 or UL 96A, as applicable.

Cross-references: NFPA 70; NFPA 780; UL 96A.

title: Interconnection of systems
text:
Interconnection of systems. All lightning protection systems on a building or structure shall be interconnected in accordance with NFPA 780 or UL 96A, as applicable.

Cross-references: NFPA 780; UL 96A.
